message:
fix(runtime_compat): bind select! arms to locals before pin_mut to accept arbitrary expressions

The two-branch select! shims in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs
expanded $fut1 / $fut2 directly into both pin_mut!(...) and
futures::future::select(..., ...). That works when the caller passes an
identifier (pin_mut! from futures shadows the binding in place), but fails
when the caller passes an arbitrary expression such as
  select! {
      res = stream.next() => ...,
      _ = ctl.cancelled() => ...,
  }
because pin_mut! requires a local name to shadow and select(expr, expr) would
re-evaluate any side-effectful expression twice.

Fix by lifting each arm's future into a named local (__ft_select_fut1 /
__ft_select_fut2) before pinning, so pin_mut! always sees an identifier and
select() borrows the pinned locals. The naming uses __ft_select_fut{1,2} to
avoid colliding with any user identifier in the calling scope (macro hygiene
for these declarative macros is only shadow-based, not gensym).

Applied to both macro arms:
  - block-body form: $pat1:pat = $fut1:expr => $body1:block ...
  - expression-body form: $pat1:pat = $fut1:expr => $body1:expr ...

Same file: strip trailing blank lines inside function/block bodies that
clippy::needless-blank-lines-after-last-statement-in-block flagged (cosmetic;
zero behavior change — trimming empty lines before the closing brace in
spawn_blocking-via-asupersync, mpsc_recv_option, mpsc_send,
mpsc_reserve_send, watch_has_changed, watch_borrow_and_update_clone,
watch_changed, oneshot_recv, and the disconnected-send test block).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
